demographics.text:
ADMISSION NOTE
==============
Patient Name: Sofia Ramirez
MRN: MRN-671209
Date of Birth: 07/04/1989
Age: 34 years old
Gender: Female

Admission Date: 05/15/2024

ADMITTING DIAGNOSIS: Acute Gastroenteritis

ALLERGIES: 

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Patient presents with acute gastroenteritis.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
34F presenting with 12h acute onset RLQ pain, nausea, vomiting. Vital signs stable. WBC 11.2. Surgical consult obtained � equivocal exam. CT abdomen/pelvis ordered but result pending at time of discharge. Tolerated PO challenge. Patient elected to leave.

ADMISSION MEDICATIONS:
None on file

VITAL SIGNS ON ADMISSION:
Blood Pressure: 138/88 mmHg
Heart Rate: 96 bpm
Temperature: 37.8 C
Respiratory Rate: 18/min
O2 Saturation: 97% on room air

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
General: Alert and oriented x3. Mildly distressed.
Pertinent findings per admitting note.

PLAN:
- IV access
- IV fluids 1L NS
- CT abdomen/pelvis (pending result)
- Surgical consultation

Signed: Dr. [Admitting Physician]

PROGRESS NOTE � Day 2
======================
Patient: Sofia Ramirez    MRN: MRN-671209
Date: 05/16/2024

SUBJECTIVE:
Patient reports improved symptoms. Tolerating medications.

OBJECTIVE:
Vitals: BP 132/80, HR 88, Temp 37.2, RR 16, O2 Sat 98% RA

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
Discharge Diagnosis: Acute Gastroenteritis



Plan:
- Continue current medications
- Monitor labs
- CT abdomen/pelvis � ordered, result PENDING
- Beta-hCG � ordered (rule out ectopic) � PENDING


Signed: Dr. [Covering Physician]

DISCHARGE SUMMARY
=================
Patient Name: Sofia Ramirez
MRN: MRN-671209
Date of Birth: 07/04/1989
Admission Date: 05/15/2024
Discharge Date: 05/16/2024

PRINCIPAL DIAGNOSIS: Acute Gastroenteritis vs. Early Appendicitis � see surgical note

SECONDARY DIAGNOSES:
- None documented

DISCHARGE CONDITION: 

ALLERGIES: 

HOSPITAL COURSE:
34F presenting with 12h acute onset RLQ pain, nausea, vomiting. Vital signs stable. WBC 11.2. Surgical consult obtained � equivocal exam. CT abdomen/pelvis ordered but result pending at time of discharge. Tolerated PO challenge. Patient elected to leave.

PROCEDURES PERFORMED:
- IV access
- IV fluids 1L NS
- CT abdomen/pelvis (pending result)
- Surgical consultation


DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:
- Ondansetron 4 mg PO Q8H PRN nausea
- Ibuprofen 400 mg PO Q6H PRN pain


PENDING RESULTS AT DISCHARGE:
- CT abdomen/pelvis � ordered, result PENDING (PENDING)
- Beta-hCG � ordered (rule out ectopic) � PENDING (PENDING)


FOLLOW-UP INSTRUCTIONS:
- Return to ED if pain worsens, fever, or unable to tolerate PO
- Follow up with PCP in 48�72 hours


Signed: Dr. [Discharging Physician]

LABORATORY RESULTS
==================
Patient: Sofia Ramirez    MRN: MRN-671209

COMPLETE BLOOD COUNT (Admission):
WBC:        11.2  [4.5-11.0]  H
Hemoglobin: 12.8  [12.0-16.0]
Hematocrit: 38.5  [36.0-48.0]
Platelets:  245   [150-400]

COMPREHENSIVE METABOLIC PANEL:
Sodium:     138   [136-145]
Potassium:  3.2   [3.5-5.1]   L
Chloride:   101   [98-107]
CO2:        18    [22-29]     L
BUN:        24    [7-20]      H
Creatinine: 1.4   [0.6-1.2]   H
Glucose:    412   [70-100]    H
eGFR:       48    mL/min      (CKD Stage 3)

PENDING RESULTS:
CT abdomen/pelvis � ordered, result PENDING
Beta-hCG � ordered (rule out ectopic) � PENDING

==================================
